meaning: n.牢牢抓住,钉紧,敲弯的钉头v.紧握,(拳头)牢牢地抓住,确定,敲弯vi.握紧,钉牢
examples: Don't clench your fists because it can lead to tightening the arms, shoulders, and neck.
They leap to their feet, clench fists and let out long screams, clawing at the ground and air.
The next time you're faced with a choice that takes self-control, clench your fist or firm that bicep.
Or just clench your jaw and hang on with white knuckles in terrified silence.
In this simple mnemonic example, the clench phase corresponds to convergence and the release to divergence.
